title: subagent: execute_task_tool uses block_in_place inside tokio runtime — can deadlock if already on current_thread runtime
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness / Performance. Location: src/subagent.rs:1275-1285 (sync branch of execute_task_tool). Violation: block_in_place only works on a multi-threaded tokio runtime. On a current_thread runtime it panics. Worse, falling through to tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() creates a NEW runtime inside an existing one — a well-known anti-pattern that can deadlock or double-spawn background tasks. The global BACKGROUND_AGENTS HashMap + TRANSCRIPT_STORE are shared across runtimes, so task registration happens on one runtime and polling on another. Mandated Refactor: Make execute_task_tool async and plumb its awaitability up to the tool dispatch layer (tools.rs execute_tool). The sync wrapper is an artifact of the tool layer being sync — fix the root cause. If that is too invasive, gate behind a runtime check and surface a clear error on single-threaded runtimes rather than silently mis-behaving.
